question: Can I bring a bag?
answer: Bags up to A4 size (30×21 cm) are allowed. Larger bags must be left at the bag drop outside Gate B (fee applies, cash-free). Clear bags speed up security checks.

question: Can I leave and re-enter the stadium?
answer: No. Once your ticket is scanned there is no re-entry until the match ends.

question: Can I bring water?
answer: Sealed plastic bottles up to 500 ml are allowed. Free water refill points are on the South Concourse and Upper Tier West.

question: Is alcohol sold in the stadium?
answer: Alcohol-free and low-alcohol beer are sold on all concourses until the 75th minute. Alcohol may not be taken to your seat in the family sections (116–122).

question: Where can I smoke?
answer: Estadio Aurora is fully smoke-free, including e-cigarettes. There is no smoking area inside the venue.

question: Do children need a ticket?
answer: Everyone, including infants, needs a valid ticket. Family restrooms with changing tables are on the South Concourse.

question: What accessibility services are available?
answer: Step-free routes reach every level via ramps and the NE/SW lifts. Gate D has the widest accessible entrance. The wheelchair platform is in Lower Bowl West (sections 123–130). Ask any steward in a yellow vest for assistance.

question: Is there a prayer room?
answer: A multi-faith Prayer & Quiet Room is on the West Concourse, near the northwest corner. It is open from gates opening until one hour after full-time.

question: Where is lost and found?
answer: Lost & Found is at the Information Desk on the North Concourse, near Gate A. Items are kept for 30 days.

question: My ticket QR code won't load. What do I do?
answer: Go to the Information Desk near Gate A with your booking reference and photo ID — staff can reissue your QR code. Screenshots are accepted at all gates.

question: Can I pay with cash?
answer: The stadium is fully cashless. All major cards and mobile wallets are accepted. A card-loading kiosk is next to the Fan Shop on the East Concourse.

question: Where do I get medical help?
answer: The First Aid Station is on the North Concourse. In an emergency, alert the nearest steward — every section has a medic assigned.

question: Can I bring an umbrella?
answer: Small folding umbrellas are allowed but may not be opened in the seating bowl. Ponchos are sold at the Fan Shop.

question: Can I bring flags and banners?
answer: Flags up to 2×1.5 m on poles shorter than 1 m are welcome. Banners must not block advertising boards or other fans' views. No political messaging.

question: How long is half-time and what should I know?
answer: Half-time is 15 minutes. Concourse food stands are busiest in the first 5 minutes — the last 5 minutes are much quieter, or order at Café Alto on the Upper Tier.
